title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser presents a framework for delegating software development tasks to AI agents while minimizing human attention overhead. The stack includes signal agents that monitor communication channels, verification gates for safety, weekly meta-analysis of conversation history for skill discovery, and biometric integration to prevent developer burnout.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS lays out a framework for delegating software development to AI agents while keeping human attention overhead low. The stack pairs signal agents that monitor communication channels with verification gates for safety, weekly meta-analysis of conversation history for skill discovery, and biometric integration meant to prevent developer burnout. It matters because it treats agent oversight as a resource to be budgeted, shifting the developer role toward supervising parallel execution rather than direct coding. Key elements include human-in-the-loop checkpoints, voice input, logic gates, conversation history mining, and parallel execution.
